description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses the node:latest Docker image and caches the node_modules/ directory. Define three stages: build, test, and deploy. In the build stage add a documents job that runs `npm install --save-dev` then `node node_modules/.bin/jsdoc -c ./.jsdoc.conf.json` and stores the generated docs/ folder as an artifact named plugin-documentation. In the test stage add a coverage job that runs `npm install --save-dev` then `node node_modules/.bin/istanbul cover ./tests/perform-tests.js coverage`, uploads the coverage/ folder as an artifact named plugin-coverage, extracts coverage with the regex '^Statements *: (\d+\.\d+%)', and runs only on the master and development branches; also add a test job that runs `npm install --save-dev` then `node ./tests/perform-tests.js` and runs on all branches except master. In the deploy stage add a pages job that moves the docs to public/, moves coverage/lcov-report/ to public/coverage, publishes the public/ directory as a GitLab Pages artifact (expires in 30 days), depends on the documents and coverage jobs, and runs only on the master branch.
